steps.title: Identify the structural contrast to emphasize
steps.content: The notes draw a clear distinction between the two materials: graphite has graphene sheets that are stacked, while carbon nanotubes have sheets wrapped into a cylindrical shape. The answer must state both structural forms directly and contrast them.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• "Composed of sheets wrapped into a cylindrical shape, carbon nanotubes contrast with graphite, a carbon allotrope composed of graphene sheets.": Describes nanotubes' cylindrical shape but omits that graphite sheets are stacked — the key contrast is missing. ✗
  • "Graphite and carbon nanotubes are allotropes composed of stacked or wrapped graphene sheets, but carbon can exist in several distinct structural forms.": Mentions "stacked or wrapped" loosely but focuses on carbon allotropes broadly, not the specific structural difference between graphite and nanotubes. ✗
  • "Unlike carbon nanotubes, which are composed of sheets of carbon atoms arranged in a flat honeycomb pattern, graphite is a cylindrical carbon allotrope.": Reverses the facts — graphite is not cylindrical, and carbon nanotubes are not flat. ✗
  • "The graphene sheets in graphite are stacked, whereas in carbon nanotubes, they form a cylinder.": States both structural forms precisely and uses "whereas" to mark the contrast directly from the notes. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
